mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window or forward returns show no statistical significance against a zero-mean null hypothesis.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative equity returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 24-month backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts confirms broad-based domestic demand growth, driving sustained positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day forward returns in at least 60% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices during periods of stable consumer prices indicate healthy capacity utilization without inflationary pressure, generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO momentum yields negative 20-day returns when CPIAUCSL month-over-month growth exceeds 0.4%.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level exceeding 2.0 percent combined with rising inflation expectations forces rapid portfolio de-leveraging, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in over 50% of instances where the trigger condition is met across a 36-month evaluation period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent monthly growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and corporate revenue potential, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS growth falls below 0.01 or forward returns show no directional bias.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising real GDP growth and declining housing starts indicates a shift toward services consumption, which historically supports positive equity valuations over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-HOUST divergence signal produces negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of historical observations.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a period of falling unemployment signals a supply-side bottleneck that compresses corporate margins and drives negative equity returns over the following week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO momentum combined with falling UNRATE fails to predict negative 5-day returns in at least 60% of triggered cases.
